Some perspective for you all. Especially those who feel like they don't have enough capital.

"Is $10mm enough to retire, I barely have $1mm etc. etc."

$700,000 compounded at 30% for 30 years is $1.8B.

It's not about the capital base, it's about the rate of return.

tweets.text:
Still believe SUI will be one of the few L1s that survives and comes out stronger on the other side of this bear market.

TVL is near ATHs in terms of SUI.

Free payments is a massive upgrade from last month that should have a compounding network effect as it has time to grow.

Native privacy is coming, and quantum resistance is already being developed / tested.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
We just shipped free tier payments. Confidential transfers are next. Walrus is becoming the memory layer for agents, and we're building native payment intents so agents can transact on their own.
Not roadmap talk but shipping, right now, while the market sleeps.

tweets.text:
Average purchase price of the Bitcoin was $65,332.

The cash raise + BTC acquisition was funded entirely by common MSTR ATM sales (as expected).

The issue:

The current adjusted mNAV is 1.2x.

According to Saylor’s own words in the Q1 Earnings Call:

"Where it used to be a 1.0x MNAV, as we add debt and as we add preferreds - primarily to our structure - the breakeven increases. Right now, it is about 1.22x. That means at 1.22x or higher MNAV, it is accretive for us to sell MSTR and buy Bitcoin. Below 1.22x MNAV, it is actually more accretive for us to sell Bitcoin and pay off our dividends..."

He’s diluting MSTR common shareholders.

And he’ll continue to do so.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Strategy has acquired 1,550 BTC for $101 million to increase our $BTC Reserve to ₿845,256. We have also increased our USD Reserve by $100 million to $1.0 billion. $MSTR $STRC https://t.co/1Zf1AVsP1H

thread_tweets.full_text:
Average purchase price of the Bitcoin was $65,332.

The cash raise + BTC acquisition was funded entirely by common MSTR ATM sales (as expected).

The issue:

The current adjusted mNAV is 1.2x.

According to Saylor’s own words in the Q1 Earnings Call:

"Where it used to be a 1.0x MNAV, as we add debt and as we add preferreds - primarily to our structure - the breakeven increases. Right now, it is about 1.22x. That means at 1.22x or higher MNAV, it is accretive for us to sell MSTR and buy Bitcoin. Below 1.22x MNAV, it is actually more accretive for us to sell Bitcoin and pay off our dividends..."

He’s diluting MSTR common shareholders.

And he’ll continue to do so.

thread_tweets.text: Not to mention they’ve added billions more in preferred since the Q1 Earnings call, so the breakeven should have moved up even higher than the 1.22 since then based on what Saylor said.

tweets.full_text:
Average purchase price of the Bitcoin was $65,332.

The cash raise + BTC acquisition was funded entirely by common MSTR ATM sales (as expected).

The issue:

The current adjusted mNAV is 1.2x.

According to Saylor’s own words in the Q1 Earnings Call:

"Where it used to be a 1.0x MNAV, as we add debt and as we add preferreds - primarily to our structure - the breakeven increases. Right now, it is about 1.22x. That means at 1.22x or higher MNAV, it is accretive for us to sell MSTR and buy Bitcoin. Below 1.22x MNAV, it is actually more accretive for us to sell Bitcoin and pay off our dividends..."

He’s diluting MSTR common shareholders.

And he’ll continue to do so.

Not to mention they’ve added billions more in preferred since the Q1 Earnings call, so the breakeven should have moved up even higher than the 1.22 since then based on what Saylor said.

“I own MSTR and I don’t care if he dilutes me” https://t.co/5mCkfdj0kR

tweets.text:
Excellent work by the Yuga team and worthwhile thread to read.

Hate to see exploits - but love to see proactive work done by good people to minimize the damage.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
A Flooring exploit today turned a dust amount of WETH into a near-infinite fpToken balance, allowing the attacker to drain Flooring pools.

This led to a followup opportunist scooping up tokens from the now depleted pools and exchanging them for underlying NFTs.

1/🧵
tweets.full_text:
Excellent work by the Yuga team and worthwhile thread to read.

Hate to see exploits - but love to see proactive work done by good people to minimize the damage.
